skill.name: marketing-psychology
skill.description: Apply psychological principles, mental models, and behavioral science to marketing strategy, messaging, pricing, and conversion work.
skill.summary: Use 70+ marketing psychology models to analyze customer behavior and improve ethical persuasion.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 26 potential issues, but all listed locations are prose in SKILL.md about marketing psychology concepts. No executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access, environment access, cryptographic functions, or prompt injection attempts were found. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from ordinary marketing terminology.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams often struggle to explain why customers hesitate, compare, or decide. This skill maps 70+ behavioral models to practical messaging, pricing, funnel, and growth decisions.
actual_capabilities: Explains marketing uses for 70+ mental models and behavioral science concepts.
Maps common marketing challenges to relevant psychological models.
Suggests ethical applications for conversion, pricing, onboarding, and retention.
Helps diagnose customer friction using decision-making and behavior models.
Provides context questions for clarifying the customer journey and desired behavior.
limitations: Does not run analytics, experiments, or market research by itself.
Does not guarantee conversion lift or revenue improvement.
Requires accurate audience, product, and funnel context from the user.
May need legal or compliance review for regulated marketing claims.

use_cases.title: Improve Landing Page Persuasion
use_cases.description: Diagnose why visitors hesitate and identify psychological models that can reduce friction and clarify value.

use_cases.title: Design Pricing Pages
use_cases.description: Apply anchoring, framing, choice architecture, and mental accounting to pricing presentation.

use_cases.title: Plan Ethical Campaign Messaging
use_cases.description: Choose persuasion principles that fit the customer journey without relying on false urgency or manipulation.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Models
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a marketing problem
prompt_templates.prompt: I am trying to improve [specific marketing outcome]. My audience is [audience], and the current obstacle is [obstacle]. Which marketing psychology models apply, and why?

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this landing page copy for psychological friction. Identify the strongest three mental models to apply, explain the reasoning, and suggest ethical changes.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Pricing Presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pricing psychology to evaluate these plans: [plans]. Recommend changes to framing, anchors, tier order, and decision clarity.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Behavior Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a psychology-informed strategy to move users from [current behavior] to [desired behavior]. Include models, journey stage, ethical guardrails, and test ideas.

output_examples.input: Our free trial signup rate is low even though traffic is qualified.
output_examples.output: The analysis may point to activation energy, status-quo bias, regret aversion, and Hick's Law. Recommended changes would reduce form effort, make the first step feel safe, and clarify what happens after signup.

output_examples.input: We need to make our annual plan more attractive without misleading customers.
output_examples.output: The response may use anchoring, mental accounting, and price relativity. It would suggest clearer savings framing, a fair comparison point, and direct explanation of who benefits from annual billing.

output_examples.input: Customers understand the product but delay purchase decisions.
output_examples.output: The skill may identify loss aversion, regret aversion, social proof, and goal-gradient effects. It would recommend reducing perceived risk and showing credible evidence near the decision point.

best_practices: Provide the audience, offer, journey stage, and desired behavior before asking for recommendations.
Ask for ethical guardrails when applying urgency, scarcity, defaults, or social proof.
Turn recommendations into testable hypotheses before changing a full campaign.
anti_patterns: Do not use fake scarcity, false social proof, or misleading urgency.
Do not apply every mental model at once to a single page or campaign.
Do not replace customer research with theory alone.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps apply behavioral science and mental models to marketing strategy, copy, pricing, funnels, and retention.

faq.question: Is this only for conversion optimization?
faq.answer: No. It also supports positioning, campaign planning, pricing, onboarding, trust building, and growth strategy.

faq.question: Does it create finished copy?
faq.answer: It can guide copy direction, but its main value is choosing and explaining the psychology behind the message.

faq.question: Can it validate whether a tactic will work?
faq.answer: No. It can form hypotheses, but real performance requires customer research, analytics, and testing.

faq.question: Does it support ethical marketing?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill repeatedly frames persuasion as something to apply ethically and with genuine customer value.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Share the target audience, offer, current customer behavior, desired behavior, journey stage, and known objections.
